name: pydicom (DICOM Medical Image I/O)

auth.notes: Local file I/O; no auth (DICOM network via pynetdicom separately)

performance.notes: File I/O dependent; pixel data decompression can be slow for large scans

agent_readiness.notes: Read/write DICOM medical image files. dcm = pydicom.dcmread('file.dcm'). Access tags: dcm.PatientName, dcm.PixelData. Pixel array: dcm.pixel_array (numpy). Modify and write back: dcm.save_as(). DICOM standard compliance. Supports all modalities: CT, MRI, X-ray, PET, US. Metadata access and modification. pixel_array → numpy for ML processing. DICOM network operations via pynetdicom companion. Required for medical imaging pipelines. Works with MONAI and SimpleITK.

gotchas: DICOM standard is complex — domain expertise required
Pixel data decompression requires additional packages (pylibjpeg, gdcm)
PHI/PII in DICOM metadata — de-identification required before ML
best_when: Reading and processing DICOM medical images for ML
Medical imaging research needing pixel data + metadata access
De-identification scripts for DICOM dataset preparation
Converting DICOM to PNG/NIfTI for downstream processing
avoid_when: Non-DICOM medical formats (use nibabel for NIfTI)
General image processing (PIL/PIL simpler for non-medical)
summary: pydicom is the standard Python library for reading and writing DICOM medical image files. dcmread() loads files, pixel_array gives numpy arrays for ML processing. Full DICOM metadata access (PatientName, modality, etc.). Required foundation for medical imaging ML pipelines. Works with MONAI and SimpleITK. Remember to de-identify PHI metadata before using in ML datasets.
